key_points: Tark doubles (Tarkington Hall) notoriously cramped, referenced as punishment
Administration accused of fitting too many students into limited infrastructure
Hillenbrand Hall notable enough to have its own campus lore
key_quotes: Ways that he can cut costs to further degrade the undergrad experience here
Fitting five students into a Tark double
They only want to use Indy to offload students from WL because there's too many students for the infrastructure

key_points: Strong sports culture dominates social scene, especially basketball
Campus has bizarre viral moments that become shared lore (WALC incidents, peanut butter man)
Frat culture present but not the only social option; nerdy subcultures also thrive
key_quotes: Welcome! But you may be surprised to find out the majority cosplayers at Purdue are for Genshin and similar gacha games
Ahh yes the 2 poles of WALC - humping the couch or pooping under the stairs
Is there anything at Purdue that's fun anymore?

key_points: Ford dining court bans quirky student traditions like 'pilk' (Pepsi + milk)
Dining courts have cult-like student followings and inside jokes
Restrictions feel increasingly corporate and fun-policing to students
key_quotes: They banned pilk at ford
I'm sorry, I thought this was America
Dang we cant waste room for paying customers. Fire the owner

key_points: Student deaths occur regularly enough that staff posts public warnings every 6-12 months
CAPS (Counseling) referenced as a resource but not praised for quality
Academic pressure from engineering/CS is a documented mental health stressor
key_quotes: About every 6 months to a year or so, we lose one of our Boilermakers to some sort of horrific incident
In retrospect it's ridiculous we weren't notified or reassured in any way
But seriously take care of yourselves and go to CAPS if you need help

key_points: University subsidizes airport flights but students note it can't afford basic campus bus service
Purdue broke its 2003 promise of permanent lifetime alumni email addresses
Administration gave top officials large raises while workers received less
key_quotes: The university cant afford bus service on campus but yet they can subsidize unprofitable flights to Purdue
I dont care if he is Chinese... I would like just to know why the guy got a huge raise, and along with a few other top officials at Purdue, but the rest of the workers got [less]
Purdue reneges on permanent lifetime email

key_points: Engineering and CS are legitimately brutal — Calc, Diff EQ, and CS240 have cult-level infamy
Widespread AI cheating crisis in CS classes led to mass academic dishonesty investigations
Professors like Turkstra are polarizing but respected in hindsight by graduates
key_quotes: Thousands of students have taken this very class with Turkstra and passed. Yes, it's a hard class
Nothing but busy work. I know how to use excel already. Why are we doing business shit?
I'm convinced the math department feeds off of this suffering

key_points: Purdue Indianapolis marketed deceptively as equivalent to West Lafayette — it isn't
Faculty fired over speech under pressure from Indiana state government
President Mung Chiang criticized for hypocrisy on Chinese national policies
key_quotes: Purdue has been blatantly lying about both the indy program and the purdue global system for a long fucking time
Shameful that Purdue is doing this and trying to market the entire experience as one and the same
The most milquetoast stance possible. Absolutely insane thing to lose your job over

key_points: West Lafayette is small and isolated — students openly say they hate living there
Campus is visually beautiful, especially at night, but surrounded by freezing winters
New airport terminal and United flights to Chicago are a genuine quality-of-life upgrade
key_quotes: I hate living in West Lafayette dawg
I miss purdue at night. I feel like I spent most of my undergrad walking between my house on salisbury street and the math building, mostly after dark
There in the yonder are a few huts in the middle of a cornfield, freezing and snowed in, and yet they must march to class... grit

key_points: Engineering and CS degrees carry real weight — recruiters show up to farmer's markets by accident
AI over-reliance flagged as a serious job-readiness problem by recent grads and employers
Top-ranked CS and engineering programs consistently place in national top 10-15
key_quotes: If you're using AI to do your homework you can just skip your first job in major and skip to the unemployment lines
I see it everyday in the new grads at the office and it's a huge problem
I went over to Pepsi to get some Pepsi - they started asking me for a resume? And why were there aeroplane companies there?

key_points: Out-of-state cost (~$47k/year) makes value proposition weaker vs. in-state alternatives
Purdue Global and Purdue Indianapolis use the Purdue brand to charge near-flagship prices
For in-state engineering students, widely considered one of the best public school values
key_quotes: I accidentally applied to Purdue Indianapolis instead of the WL campus, completed my degree, and graduated
Should have applied to Chicken. You're cooked
At Purdue University... the cost of attendance is 47k/year

key_points: Intense academic culture with a dark humor coping mechanism — suffering is a shared identity
Basketball brings campus together; football is a running joke students accept with grace
Gritty, nerdy, proud — alumni affection is genuine and strong despite the suffering
key_quotes: To be a fan is to suffer
Welcome to the club, friend. You slowly realise that you won't make memories like the ones from here again
Once a Boiler, always a Boiler

key_points: Safety: student deaths occur every 6-12 months; notification protocols called inadequate
Institutional Honesty: Purdue Indianapolis actively misrepresented as equivalent to West Lafayette
Civil Liberties: faculty fired for speech, Chinese grad students effectively banned, ICE detained a student
key_quotes: About every 6 months to a year or so, we lose one of our Boilermakers to some sort of horrific incident
Purdue effectively bans grad students from China, other countries, faculty say
Purdue University accused of 'chilling' retaliation for sexual assault reports

key_points: Mackey Arena basketball atmosphere is genuinely elite — students are obsessed
Campus photography is stunning, especially winter nights near the bell tower
New direct flights from the Amelia Earhart Terminal to Chicago O'Hare are a real convenience win
key_quotes: Easily one of the best pictures I've seen of campus
It looks like a movie set. Amazing
God I hope this is super successful and we get another airline doing flights to places like Indianapolis
